mappings.rationaleAI: This article directly describes Earth's radially layered interior — crust (oceanic and continental), asthenosphere, mantle, liquid outer core, and solid inner core — covering the full scope of outline paths A.1 and A.2. It also covers the seismic wave analysis (P and S waves) and gravity/magnetic measurements used to infer interior structure, directly addressing D.1 and D.1.a.

Earthquakes are the primary subject of outline section B in full: the article covers causes (B.1), geographic distribution (B.2), magnitude and elastic-rebound dynamics (B.3), and seismic measurements (B.4). Seismic waves from earthquakes are also the main geophysical evidence for inferring interior structure, linking to D.1.

Geology as a discipline encompasses petrology, structural geology, plate tectonics, and geochemical dating, covering all four major divisions of the section 213 outline as a broad parent. It provides the disciplinary framework within which Earth's interior structure, seismology, and geochemical evidence are studied.

The Earth article covers internal structure including tectonic plates, liquid outer core, and magnetosphere, directly addressing the concentric-layer model (A) and basic divisions of the solid Earth (A.2). It serves as the broad parent entry for the section's subject matter.

Chemical element covers elemental abundance in the Earth's crust and interior, connecting to outline path C (distribution of elements in Earth's core, mantle, and crust). Its coverage of meteorite composition and nucleosynthetic origins also touches D.2.b, which concerns meteorites as analogues for Earth interior composition.
